commit.message:
refactor: separar Calendar y Carousel en subpath imports opcionales

BREAKING CHANGE: Calendar, DatePicker, DateRangePicker y Carousel
ya no se exportan desde '@3df-spa/ui'. Usar subpaths:
  '@3df-spa/ui/calendar' → Calendar, DatePicker, DateRangePicker
  '@3df-spa/ui/carousel' → Carousel y sub-componentes

- Crear src/index.calendar.ts y src/index.carousel.ts como entry points
- Remover Calendar/DatePicker de forms.ts y Carousel de disclosure.ts
- Multi-entry en vite.config.ts: index + calendar + carousel
- peerDependenciesMeta: date-fns y embla-carousel como opcionales
- Nuevos exports en package.json: ./calendar, ./carousel
- CSS animaciones: ./index.css → ./ui.css

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Close Unit Test Gaps to >95% (#24)

* chore: open draft PR for unit test coverage work

* test: expand branch coverage and remove Codacy

* test: raise branch coverage in app and views

* refactor: clean up spacing and formatting in CertificationDashboard and GuidedScheduler components

Signed-off-by: Alex Hyer <theonehyer@gmail.com>

* feat(tests): enhance test coverage and add new scenarios for various components

- Add tests for email validation and trainer profile creation in AddPersonDialog
- Implement no-results message for student search in EnrollStudentsDialog
- Add support for deselecting students and handling empty states in EnrollStudentsDialog
- Enhance workload recommendations tests to handle unknown trainers
- Add fallback handling for non-numeric utilization values in RiskTrendChart
- Improve TrendChart tests for invalid date handling and empty series
- Add UtilizationChart test for invalid date parsing
- Enhance WorkloadDistribution tests for missing trainer metadata
- Update CertificationDashboard to use unique keys for certification records
- Add tooltip for burnout risk in GuidedScheduler
- Enhance ScheduleTemplates tests for undefined persisted templates
- Add checks for undefined schedules in use-check-in-scheduler
- Enhance risk history tests for fallback structures
- Add session frequency and course variety recommendations in burnout analytics
- Enhance certification tracker tests for renewal notices and compliance summaries
- Add scheduler tests for overlapping availability and conflict detection
- Enhance wellness analytics tests for mood insights and recovery recommendations
- Update Vite config to increase branch coverage threshold

chore: release 0.1.6

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

docs(docs): clarify issue assignment requirement before starting work (#299)

Clarify issue assignment requirement before starting work. Two
contributors independently opened PRs for the same issue (#288 → #293
and #294) because the contribution workflow didn't require issue
assignment before starting work.

Add step 1 to PR Workflow in
`docs/contributing/contribution-workflow.md`: "Check the issue is open
and unassigned" — request assignment via comment before starting work.
Covers both "already assigned" and `needs-triage` edge cases. Renumbers
existing steps 2–9 and updates `ms.date`.

The new step reads:

```markdown
1. **Check the issue is open and unassigned.** Comment on the issue to request assignment before starting any work. Maintainers will assign you when confirmed.
   * If the issue is already assigned to someone, do not open a competing PR without first coordinating with the assignee or a maintainer.
   * Issues labelled `needs-triage` are not ready to be picked up. You are welcome to comment your interest in being assigned — maintainers will follow up once triage is complete.
```

## Type of Change

- [x] 📚 Documentation update

## Component(s) Affected

- [x] `docs/` - Documentation

## Testing Performed

- [x] `npm run lint:md` passes with 0 errors
- [x] `cspell` spell check passes with 0 issues

## Documentation Impact

- [x] Documentation updated in this PR

## Checklist

- [x] My code follows the [project conventions](copilot-instructions.md)
- [x] Commit messages follow [conventional commit
format](instructions/commit-message.instructions.md)
- [x] I have performed a self-review
- [x] Documentation impact assessed above
- [x] No new linting warnings introduced

Closes #295
---------

chore: support auto-closed release PRs in tag workflow (#7396)

* chore: support auto-closed release PRs in tag workflow

When pushing directly to main from a release branch, GitHub auto-closes
the associated PR without marking it as merged. Relax the gate condition
to also trigger for closed release-branch PRs, with a safety check that
verifies the PR's head commit is actually in main before proceeding.

Co-authored-by: TheOneHyer <8741979+TheOneHyer@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix plugin resolution in npm/yarn/pnpm monorepo workspaces (#426)

* Initial plan

* fix: resolve plugins from ancestor node_modules in monorepo workspaces

Walk up the entire directory tree checking each node_modules for the
plugin instead of stopping at the first package.json. This supports npm
workspaces and other monorepo setups where packages are hoisted to the
root node_modules rather than the workspace's own node_modules.

Co-authored-by: philmillman <3722211+philmillman@users.noreply.github.com>

* refactor: introduce WorkspaceInfo with lazy detection via AsyncLocalStorage

Replace detectJsPackageManager with a broader detectWorkspaceInfo that
also returns the workspace root path, isMonorepo flag, and monorepo tool.
Workspace detection is deferred until first access via a memoized getter
stored in AsyncLocalStorage, so no fs work occurs for commands that don't
use @plugin decorators.

Use the workspace rootPath in plugin resolution to stop walking node_modules
ancestors at the project boundary instead of the filesystem root.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: stop workspace detection walk at .git root as fallback boundary

When no lockfile is found, stop walking up the directory tree when a
.git folder is encountered rather than continuing to the filesystem root.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: philmillman <3722211+philmillman@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Theo Ephraim <theo@dmno.dev>
Co-authored-by: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat: add `ifs()` function and update `remap()` to positional arg pairs (legacy syntax deprecated) (#421)

* Initial plan

* feat: add ifs() function and update remap() to use positional args

- Add new ifs() resolver that evaluates condition/value pairs (like Excel's IFS)
- Update remap() to use positional args: remap($VAR, match, val, default?)
  instead of key=value pairs
- Update simple-resolver.ts in env-spec-parser for new remap syntax
- Update tests for new remap syntax and add ifs() tests
- Update all docs and examples to use new remap syntax"

Co-authored-by: theoephraim <1158956+theoephraim@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: remove accidentally committed symlinks from packages/utils

Co-authored-by: theoephraim <1158956+theoephraim@users.noreply.github.com>

* refactor: add clarifying comments to remap() and ifs() loop logic

Co-authored-by: theoephraim <1158956+theoephraim@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: support legacy key=value remap syntax (deprecated) and add changeset

- remap() now supports both positional pairs (new preferred) and key=value pairs (deprecated)
- Add deprecation warning to console when old syntax is used
- Add changeset for minor version bump
- Update docs to show deprecated notice for old syntax

Co-authored-by: theoephraim <1158956+theoephraim@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: replace console.warn with item-level deprecation warning in varlock load output

- Remove console.warn from RemapResolver legacy mode
- Add SchemaError(isWarning=true) with tip to _schemaErrors so it shows in
  `varlock load` pretty output as [WARNING] below the affected item
- Fix config-item.ts and env-graph.ts to not block resolution for warning-only errors
- Update test helper to support `expectWarnings` flag for deprecated-but-working syntax

Co-authored-by: theoephraim <1158956+theoephraim@users.noreply.github.com>

* Update .changeset/add-ifs-function-update-remap.md

simple resolver is only used for our tests

* Apply suggestion from @theoephraim

commit.message:
Add USDA lookup and web search LLM tools, update system prompt for recipe assembly

- Add lookup_usda tool: wraps existing USDA client as an LLM-callable tool
  for looking up raw/generic food nutrition (per 100g)
- Add search_web_nutrition tool: DuckDuckGo HTML search for branded, fast
  food, and restaurant nutrition info (returns snippets for LLM to parse)
- Update tool executor in parse.py to support async handlers
- Update system prompt: fix stale get_foods/get_recipes refs to
  get_foods_and_recipes, add nutrition lookup workflow (DB → USDA → web),
  add recipe assembly workflow from pasted recipes
- Register both new tools in NUTRITION_TOOL_DEFINITIONS/HANDLERS

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Use lightweight refresh for meal operations instead of full page reload

Add refreshMeals() that only re-fetches the daily summary without
setting loading=true or re-fetching trends/workouts. All meal
operations (delete, add, edit grams, change type, quick-add) now
use this instead of refreshTrends(), eliminating the full-page
repaint flash.

commit.message:
Automerge: [libclc][NFC] Change include style from <...> to "..." (#186537)

project-specific headers should use "". Keep #include <amdhsa_abi.h>

llvm-diff shows no change to libclc.bc for spir--, spir64--, nvptx64--,
nvptx64--nvidiacl, nvptx64-nvidia-cuda and amdgcn-amd-amdhsa-llvm when
LIBCLC_TARGETS_TO_BUILD is "all".
Verified that reversing spir64--/libclc.spv and spir--/libclc.spv to
LLVM bitcode shows no diff.

Also fix `__CLC_INTEGER_CLC_BITFIELD_EXTRACT_SIGNED_H__` guard per
copilot review.

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot Autofix powered by AI <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(tun): use async sleep to wait for TUN interface visibility before route setup (#1060)

* Initial plan

* fix(tun): wait for tun visibility using async sleep before adding routes

Fixes #1032 - intermittent TUN startup crash "tun interface not found".

After build_async() creates a new TUN device, the interface may not be
immediately visible via NetworkInterface::show(). This caused maybe_add_routes()
to call get_interface_by_name() -> None -> .expect() -> panic (SIGABRT).

Changes:
- Add Clone derive to TunConfig (config/internal/config.rs)
- Replace .expect("tun interface not found") with proper error return in
  maybe_add_routes (routes/mod.rs)
- Make new_internal an async associated function taking &TunConfig; add
  retry loop (up to 40x50ms = 2s) using tokio::time::sleep (not
  std::thread::sleep which blocks Tokio runtime) to wait for TUN
  interface visibility before calling maybe_add_routes (runner.rs)
- Update run_async to clone cfg and await new_internal inside tokio::spawn

Co-authored-by: ibigbug <543405+ibigbug@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(tun): address review comments - fix poll loop and surface NetworkInterface errors

- Restructure TUN visibility poll loop to avoid sleeping after the
  last attempt, eliminating false-negative window where interface
  becomes visible during the final sleep but still errors
- Capture NetworkInterface::show() errors and include the last error
  message in the Error::Operation return for better diagnostics
- Fix fmt/clippy issues in runner.rs

Co-authored-by: ibigbug <543405+ibigbug@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(tun): fix nightly fmt - adjust line break in format string

Co-authored-by: ibigbug <543405+ibigbug@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: ibigbug <543405+ibigbug@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: add preflight GPU & backend environment check

Add a standalone binary (3dgs-preflight) and container (Dockerfile.preflight)
that validates GPU capabilities and backend readiness before long-running
3DGS training jobs.

Binary changes:
- src/bin/preflight.rs: standalone CLI that detects GPU (CUDA/Metal/ROCm),
  probes external tools (nvidia-smi, python3, ffmpeg, colmap, gsplat), and
  evaluates whether a desired backend can run. Supports --expect <backend>
  to assert compatibility with numbered failure reasons on mismatch.
- Cargo.toml: add [[bin]] target for 3dgs-preflight
- src/backends/gpu_detect.rs: add Display impl for GpuPlatform

Container:
- Dockerfile.preflight: lightweight multi-stage image (~800MB) with only
  the tools needed for environment probing (no PyTorch/gsplat bundled)

CI/CD:
- .github/workflows/e2e-tests.yml: build preflight binary and run
  "--expect mock" gate before E2E tests; add multi-arch preflight
  image build to the docker-build job

Documentation:
- docs/PREFLIGHT.md: comprehensive usage guide with CLI reference,
  exit codes, example output, CI/CD integration patterns
- .github/instructions/README.md: add entry pointing to PREFLIGHT.md
- docs/DOCKER_BUILD.md: cross-reference preflight doc

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: 3DGS-accelerator
repository.description: Containerized Rust service for processing multi-view video captures into 3D Gaussian Splatting models - videos to splats
